summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the traditional name for Fe²⁺?

Ferrous is correct. This is a key concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the traditional name for Cu²⁺ compounds?

Cupric. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the traditional name for Fe³⁺?

Ferric. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the traditional name for SnCl₂?

Stannous chloride. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the modern (Stock) name for PbCl₄?

Lead(IV) chloride. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the traditional name for FeCl₂?

Ferrous chloride. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the Latin root for tin used in traditional naming?

Stannum. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the Latin root for lead?

Plumbum. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the traditional name for SnO₂?

Stannic oxide. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the modern name for the compound traditionally called 'cupric sulfate'?

Copper(II) sulfate. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the traditional name for PbO?

Plumbous oxide. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the name for CuSO₄?

Copper(II) sulfate. This is an important concept in Common Exceptions & Traditional Names.

What is the traditional name for NaHCO₃?

NaHCO₃ = sodium bicarbonate (IUPAC name) = baking soda (common name). Used as a leavening agent.

What is the traditional name for Na₂CO₃?

Na₂CO₃ = sodium carbonate = washing soda. Used in glass manufacturing and as a cleaning agent.
